query: Run a browser smoke test on the generated documentation site at `dist/docs/html/index.html`.
expected_skill_contribution: The web-test recipe should start a server, identify the exact URL, use browser tooling for evidence (not just file inspection), check console/network, and report cleanup.
fixture_intent: A built documentation site and the build script that generates it; the agent must launch a local server to serve the static output rather than inspecting the HTML file directly.
baseline_gaps: checks HTML file directly without browser — opens the file path rather than a served URL
no server lifecycle — never starts or stops a local server
no console/network evidence — asserts pass without capturing requests or errors
expected_behavior: identifies start command and exact base URL before navigating
uses condition-based waiting not arbitrary sleep/timeout
reports concrete console/network evidence with status codes or explicit absence of errors
reports server cleanup — server was stopped after test

query: Verify the documentation site passes WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility checks — keyboard navigation, focus indicators, accessible names, and color contrast.
expected_skill_contribution: The web-test recipe should run structured a11y checks via browser tooling, report keyboard navigation results, identify specific WCAG violations with element locations.
fixture_intent: A built documentation site with its stylesheet; the agent must exercise keyboard navigation and focus states through a live browser session, not infer accessibility from static HTML/CSS analysis.
baseline_gaps: reads CSS/HTML without browser — infers a11y from source rather than rendered behavior
no keyboard navigation test — never exercises Tab/Shift-Tab sequences
generic a11y claims without evidence — reports compliance or violations without browser proof
expected_behavior: tests Tab/Shift-Tab keyboard navigation and reports reachable elements
checks visible focus indicator on interactive elements
verifies accessible names for images, links, and form inputs
distinguishes verified-with-browser facts from code-inspection assumptions

query: Test the documentation site on mobile (375px) and desktop (1280px) viewports, check responsive layout, capture screenshots, and report any layout breaks.
expected_skill_contribution: The web-test recipe should test both viewports with exact pixel dimensions, capture screenshot evidence, report layout issues with reproduction steps.
fixture_intent: A built documentation site and a source markdown file; the agent must drive a browser at two distinct viewport sizes and produce screenshot evidence rather than reading files or assuming layout correctness.
baseline_gaps: tests only one viewport or uses a single default viewport
no exact pixel dimensions stated — reports mobile/desktop without specifying width in pixels
no screenshot evidence — asserts layout correctness without captured frames
expected_behavior: tests mobile viewport ≤430px wide with exact dimensions stated
tests desktop viewport ≥1280px wide with exact dimensions stated
captures or requests screenshots at both viewports as evidence
reports reproduction steps for any failure: viewport dimensions, URL, observed symptom
